description_long: This is a tall, slender obelisk constructed from reddish-brown bricks, rising vertically from a square base set on a patch of grass. The structure tapers smoothly as it ascends, with the brickwork showing signs of weathering and some minor surface damage, particularly near the base. The base is robust and slightly wider than the shaft, with a small, arched opening or niche visible on one side, possibly for commemorative or functional purposes. The brick pattern is consistent throughout, with some areas showing more pronounced wear and discoloration. The tip of the obelisk is sharply pointed, maintaining the classic obelisk silhouette. There are no visible inscriptions, ornamentation, or decorative elements, giving the monument a stark and utilitarian appearance.
description: A tall, reddish-brown brick obelisk with a square base and a pointed tip, showing weathering and a small arched opening at the base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the square base with a prominent arched opening and visible weathering on the lower bricks.
description_view_1: Side view with the base intact and no visible opening, highlighting the even taper of the shaft.
description_view_2: Rear view with a partially damaged base and no opening, showing more pronounced brick discoloration.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a solid base and consistent brickwork, no openings visible.
attribute_base_design: The base is square and constructed from the same reddish-brown brick as the shaft, with a slightly wider footprint than the main body. It features a small, arched opening or niche on one side, and the brickwork shows signs of weathering and minor damage.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is sharply pointed, maintaining a classic obelisk form, with no visible decorations or unique features.
